notes: Holmes sends Watson to Dartmoor as Henry's companion and protector while he continues his own investigation in London. Watson becomes Henry's closest ally on the moor — accompanying him to Baskerville Hall, observing the neighbors, and sending Holmes detailed reports. The relationship is that of a professional guardian and a frightened client: Watson is loyal to Holmes's instructions while becoming genuinely fond of Henry's directness and courage. Henry is one of Watson's more substantial solo-narrative responsibilities in the entire canon.
